examples: Can you help me carry this? It's really heavy.
Use the scales to measure how heavy it is.
When he was a child he was rather heavy, but today he is impressively fit.

definitions.text: In a heavy manner; weightily; heavily; gravely.
examples: Heavy-laden with their sins, time hung heavy

definitions.text: A villain or bad guy; the one responsible for evil or aggressive acts.
examples: With his wrinkled, uneven face, the actor always seemed to play the heavy in films.
A fight started outside the bar but the heavies came out and stopped it.
They piled their goods on the donkey's back, heavying up an already backbreaking load.

definitions.text: To sadden. (Can we add an example for this sense?)
To use power or wealth to exert influence on, e.g., governments or corporations; to pressure.
examples: The union was well known for the methods it used to heavy many businesses.
